grounding_note: Users say 'protect my idea/work/brand' without knowing patent, trademark, and copyright are different systems. Row 299 (inventor/patent) and row 126 (nonprofit royalties splitting Trademark/Copyright vs IP Entertainment) show even expert raters need the subject-matter fact. What the thing IS — invention, name/logo, creative work, software, performance royalties — is a fact every user can state when asked.

scenarios.opening_query: I need legal help protecting a project I've been developing for the past two years.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a new water-filtration machine — I need a patent application filed before I show it publicly.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a machine — a new water-filtration device — and I need a patent before showing it publicly.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the thing to protect is an invention, a brand, or a creative work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a series of illustrated children's books — I need the copyright side handled before pitching publishers.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's illustrated children's books — I need copyright protection before pitching publishers.
scenarios.vetting_note: v1's x0584 rebuilt; public-disclosure deadline gives the patent route real urgency.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone is stealing my work and selling it as their own. I want to stop them now.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only) || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'work' is my photography — they're printing my photos on products and selling them online.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my photography — they print my photos on products and sell them online.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what kind of work is being copied
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'work' is my patented tool design — a competitor is manufacturing knockoffs of the mechanism I patented.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my patented tool — a competitor is making knockoffs of the patented mechanism.
scenarios.vetting_note: Infringement framing without subject matter; the medium (photos vs patented mechanism) routes it.

scenarios.opening_query: Another company just started using something that belongs to my business and customers are confused.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: They registered a domain almost identical to ours and mirror our site to intercept our customers — it's a domain/website hijacking problem.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They registered a nearly identical domain and mirror our site to intercept customers — it's a domain hijacking problem.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the misuse is a brand mark or a domain/internet scheme
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're using our exact logo and name on their trucks and storefront across town.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They put our exact logo and name on their trucks and storefront.
scenarios.vetting_note: Domain-dispute vs classic mark infringement; the taxonomy's Computer Law label names domain disputes explicitly.

scenarios.opening_query: Our band needs legal help with money we're owed for our music.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Entertainment || Business and Corporate > Entertainment || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's our record deal — the label's contract lets them recoup everything and we've never seen a statement; we need the agreement renegotiated.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the record deal — their contract recoups everything and we've never seen a statement. We need it renegotiated.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is the business contract or the copyrights/royalty rights themselves
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: A streaming service is using our recordings without any license at all — pure unauthorized use of the songs.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A streaming service uses our recordings with no license at all.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 126's split: taxonomy distinguishes the business agreement behind the art from the IP itself.

scenarios.opening_query: I invented something at my kitchen table that actually works and I'm terrified of losing it.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only) || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The patent's already filed — what I need now is the licensing deal reviewed; a manufacturer sent a 40-page agreement.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The patent's filed — now a manufacturer sent a 40-page licensing agreement I need reviewed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether patent prosecution or a licensing contract is the current need
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's filed — I need the patent application itself before my crowdfunding launch.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's filed — I need the patent application done before my crowdfunding launch.
scenarios.vetting_note: Filing-status fact separates USPTO prosecution from contract review.

scenarios.opening_query: I wrote software for a client and now they claim they own everything I've ever built.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: It all turns on my consulting agreement's work-for-hire clause — I need the contract analyzed before responding to their demand letter.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It comes down to the work-for-hire clause in my consulting agreement — I need that contract analyzed before answering their demand.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the ownership fight is contractual or an infringement dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no contract at all — they copied my code repository wholesale after I refused their lowball offer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No contract exists — they copied my whole code repo after I refused their offer.
scenarios.vetting_note: Contract-vs-infringement posture; developers genuinely arrive unsure which fight they're in.

scenarios.opening_query: My small business name is being used by a bigger company and they told ME to stop using it.
The courts are interfering with my ability to develop my invention and I need someone to protect my livelihood.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only) || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'interference' is an injunction from my ex-business partner's lawsuit — until that civil case is resolved, no patent work matters.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The interference is an injunction from my ex-partner's lawsuit — that civil case has to be resolved before any patent work matters.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an underlying civil case blocks the inventor rather than a patent-office issue
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no lawsuit — I need a registered patent attorney to draft and file the application itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No lawsuit — I just need a registered patent attorney to file the application.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 299 verbatim ('courts interfering with my patent'): the humans split between GL and Patent on exactly this.

scenarios.opening_query: A publisher wants my novel but the contract they sent has clauses my writer friends say are dangerous.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > Publishing
scenarios.hidden_fact: The copyright is mine and registered — what needs work is the publishing agreement itself: rights reversion, royalties, and the option clause.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The copyright's registered and mine — it's the publishing contract that needs work: reversion, royalties, the option clause.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the need is copyright protection or publishing-contract negotiation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Beyond the contract, I discovered a self-published knockoff of my manuscript already selling online — someone leaked my draft.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Worse than the contract — someone leaked my draft and a knockoff is already selling online.
scenarios.vetting_note: Publishing has its own business label; registered-copyright status isolates the contract need.

scenarios.opening_query: I make and sell my own products at markets and a company at a trade show copied me.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.hidden_fact: What they copied is the folding mechanism I designed — the functional part, not my name or designs, and I never patented it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They copied my folding mechanism — the functional part, not my branding. I never patented it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the copied element is functional (patent) or brand/creative (trademark/copyright)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They copied my brand name and label art almost exactly — the product inside is their own.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They copied my name and label art — the product itself is theirs.
scenarios.vetting_note: Functional-vs-brand copying is THE patent/trademark boundary, stated in user-plain terms.
